title: history.recent() passes negative LIMIT to SQLite returning all rows
description: cli/src/history.rs:115: `recent(limit: i64)` binds the limit directly to SQLite's LIMIT clause with no validation. SQLite treats negative LIMIT as 'no limit', so `recent(-1)` returns the entire message table. A user running `nie log -n -1` (if the CLI accepts negative values) receives all messages instead of an error. Fix: validate `limit > 0` at the start of `recent()` and return an error on zero or negative values.
